findings.title: DOVER, FasterVQA, E*warp metric scripts depend on external code not in repo
findings.quote: from DOVER.evaluate_a_set_of_videos import evaluate_set as dover
findings.claim: eval_dover.py imports a DOVER package, eval_vbench.py imports VBench/evaluate, and eval_ewarp.py imports an ewarp module; none of DOVER/, VBench/, or ewarp.py exist anywhere in the repo (verified repo-wide in _audit_code/out/eval_artefacts.csv), and FasterVQA has no calling script at all.
findings.concern: DOVER, FasterVQA, and E*warp are reported in Table 2 for every dataset and headline the abstract/Fig. 1/Tab. 3, yet none of these numbers can be reproduced from the shipped code without third-party packages the repo neither vendors nor pins; the README's own TODO 'Add metric computation scripts for FasterVQA, DOVER, and E*warp' confirms they are unfinished.
findings.resolution: Authors: bundle or pin (with exact versions/commits) the DOVER, VBench, and RAFT/ewarp dependencies, and add a FasterVQA computation script, so Table 2's VQA and temporal-consistency columns are reproducible.
findings.paper_ref: Table 2 (FasterVQA, DOVER, E*warp rows); Table 3; Fig. 1
findings.reason: check_script confirmed DOVER, VBench, and ewarp all absent from repo (all three external_dep_present=False); import at line 150 matches the quote exactly; README TODO explicitly unfinished.

findings.title: Sec 3.3 video-processing pipeline (HQ-VSR construction) not in repo
findings.quote:
Step 1: Metadata Filtering. ... Step 2: Scene Filtering. ... Step 3: Quality
Filtering. ... Step 4: Motion Processing. ... we introduce the motion area
detection algorithm for localized processing
findings.claim: The paper describes a four-step pipeline (metadata/scene/quality/motion filtering with aesthetic, CLIP-IQA, FasterVQA, DOVER scoring and optical-flow motion-area cropping per Eq. 8) used to build HQ-VSR and ablated in Table 1d; a repo-wide search finds no code implementing scene detection, quality-score filtering, or motion-area cropping (only CLIP-IQA used as an eval metric).
findings.concern: Table 1d's '+Filter/+Motion' ablation and the HQ-VSR dataset construction cannot be reproduced or audited; the README TODO 'Release the video processing pipeline' is explicitly unchecked, confirming the omission.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the data-construction pipeline code (the four filtering steps and motion-area cropping) so HQ-VSR and Table 1d can be reproduced.
findings.reason: check_script found only CLIPIQA hits in eval metrics files — none of the pipeline hallmarks (scenedetect, FasterVQA, motion_mask, motion_area, optical_flow_score) appear anywhere in the repo; README TODO 'Release the video processing pipeline' is explicitly unchecked.

findings.title: eval_dover.py assigns DOVER scores with an off-by-one index
findings.quote:
for i, name in enumerate(pred_names):
    results[name] = dover_results[i-1]
findings.claim: Inside `for i, name in enumerate(pred_names)` the code indexes `dover_results[i-1]`, so the first clip (i=0) is assigned the LAST clip's score (index -1) and every clip is shifted by one relative to its name.
findings.concern: Per-clip DOVER scores are mismatched to clip names; the reported per-dataset DOVER average (Table 2/Tab. 3/Fig. 1) is computed over a misaligned mapping (the mean is unchanged, but per-sample values and any name-based aggregation are wrong, and it signals the script was not validated).
findings.resolution: Change `dover_results[i-1]` to `dover_results[i]`; confirm the order returned by `evaluate_set` matches `sorted(pred_names)`.
findings.reason: Lines 158-159 of eval_dover.py contain exactly `for i, name in enumerate(pred_names): results[name] = dover_results[i-1]`; check_script confirms the pattern at line 159 (eval_dover_off_by_one_line=True); off-by-one is real.

findings.title: eval_ewarp.py chdirs to script dir and uses a non-existent default RAFT path
findings.quote:
original_dir = os.getcwd()
raft_dir = os.path.join(original_dir, "RAFT")
os.chdir(raft_dir)
from ewarp import Ewarp as Ewarp
results, avg_score = Ewarp(args)
findings.claim: The script chdirs into `<script_dir>/RAFT` and imports `ewarp`, but no `RAFT/` subdirectory or `ewarp.py` exists under finetune/scripts (RAFT lives at finetune/utils/RAFT and has no ewarp.py); its default `--model finetune/scripts/models/raft-things.pth` also points to a path that does not exist (the weight is at finetune/utils/RAFT/raft-things.pth).
findings.concern: eval_ewarp.py cannot run as shipped, so the E*warp temporal-consistency column of Table 2 (a headline 'excellent temporal consistency' claim) is not reproducible from the repo.
findings.resolution: Authors: vendor the `ewarp` implementation, fix the chdir target to the real RAFT location, and correct the default `--model` path.
findings.reason: Lines 146-150 of eval_ewarp.py match the quote exactly; check_script confirms ewarp absent (external_dep_present=False) and default model path missing (eval_ewarp_default_model_exists=False); note the claim says 'script_dir' but code uses os.getcwd() — the core bug (no RAFT dir or ewarp.py anywhere, wrong default model path) is confirmed regardless.

findings.title: inference.sh passes UDM10 ground truth for every dataset's eval
findings.claim: All six eval_metrics.py invocations in inference.sh hardcode `--gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT`, even for SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, MVSR4x, and VideoLQ (verified: 6/6 --gt lines point to UDM10/GT in _audit_code/out/eval_artefacts.csv).
findings.concern: Run verbatim, the reference-based metrics (PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/DISTS) for the five non-UDM10 datasets are computed against the wrong ground truth; eval_metrics.py silently skips clips whose names lack a matching GT file (eval_metrics.py:163-179), so most clips would be dropped, producing meaningless Table 2 fidelity numbers unless the user edits the script.
findings.resolution: Authors: set `--gt` to each dataset's own GT directory in inference.sh.
findings.reason: inference.sh lines 34-37 match the quote exactly; direct inspection confirms all 6 eval_metrics.py calls (UDM10, SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, MVSR4x, VideoLQ) hardcode `--gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT`; check_script confirms 6/6 (inference_sh_gt_all_udm10=True).
